machinery_genes.role: outer-membrane secretin ring (basal body OM ring)
machinery_genes.quote: The major constituent of the OM rings of the basal body is the secretin protein MxiD
machinery_genes.note: Schroeder & Hilbi 2008 review of the S. flexneri Mxi-Spa T3SS. MxiD = SctC/YscC-family secretin; originally characterized by Allaoui et al 1993 (PMID 8437520) as 'an essential component of the Ipa secretion apparatus', homologous to YscC.

machinery_genes.quote: Membrane insertion and stabilization of the homo-oligomeric MxiD ring is essentially mediated by the lipoprotein MxiM
machinery_genes.note: MxiM = pilot protein for secretin assembly (pilotin slot in the apparatus brief, MxiM column).

machinery_genes.quote: Periplasmic and IM rings consist of the lipoprotein MxiJ and the membrane protein MxiG
machinery_genes.note: MxiG = SctD-family IM/MS-ring membrane protein of the Mxi-Spa basal body.

machinery_genes.quote: Periplasmic and IM rings consist of the lipoprotein MxiJ and the membrane protein MxiG
machinery_genes.note: MxiJ = SctJ-family inner-ring lipoprotein, partners MxiG in the basal body rings.

machinery_genes.quote: The needle is composed of the major subunit MxiH and the minor component MxiI
machinery_genes.note: MxiH = SctF-family needle filament subunit; crystal structure and needle model in Deane et al 2006 (PMID 16888041).

machinery_genes.quote: The needle is composed of the major subunit MxiH and the minor component MxiI
machinery_genes.note: MxiI = SctI-family inner-rod protein; Jouihri et al 2003 (PMID 12864857) show MxiH and MxiI transit the apparatus and are degraded if they cannot.

machinery_genes.quote: the Mxi-Spa proteins MxiA, Spa9, Spa24, Spa29, and Spa40 are proposed to be associated with the IM ring
machinery_genes.note: MxiA = SctV/InvA/YscV-family central export-gate ring; its overexpressed cytoplasmic domain shuts off secretion (Cherradi et al 2013, PMID 24126350).

machinery_genes.quote: the Mxi-Spa proteins MxiA, Spa9, Spa24, Spa29, and Spa40 are proposed to be associated with the IM ring
machinery_genes.note: Spa24 = SpaP/SctR-family export-gate protein (per apparatus brief Spa24 column).

machinery_genes.quote: the Mxi-Spa proteins MxiA, Spa9, Spa24, Spa29, and Spa40 are proposed to be associated with the IM ring
machinery_genes.note: Spa9 = SpaQ/SctS-family export-gate protein (per apparatus brief Spa9 column).

machinery_genes.quote: the Mxi-Spa proteins MxiA, Spa9, Spa24, Spa29, and Spa40 are proposed to be associated with the IM ring
machinery_genes.note: Spa29 = SpaR/SctT-family export-gate protein (per apparatus brief Spa29 column).

machinery_genes.quote: Spa13 interacts with the cleaved form of Spa40 and with the translocator chaperone IpgC, suggesting that Spa13 intervenes during the secretion hierarchy switch process
machinery_genes.note: Spa40 = SpaS/YscU/SctU-family export-gate autoprotease; the cleaved form drives the secretion hierarchy switch. Also listed among IM-ring Mxi-Spa proteins in Schroeder & Hilbi 2008 (PMID 18202440).

machinery_genes.quote: The assembly of the T3SS needle and the subsequent effector secretion also depend on the C-ring ATPase Spa47
machinery_genes.note: Spa47 = SctN/InvC/YscN-family T3SS ATPase; Jouihri et al 2003 (PMID 12864857) place it in the cytoplasmic Spa47-Spa33-MxiK-MxiN module.

machinery_genes.quote: The backbone of this cytoplasmic interface of the secretion apparatus, also called the C ring, is the Spa33 protein
machinery_genes.note: Spa33 = SctQ/SpaO-family C-ring protein; interacts with Spa47, MxiK and MxiN (Jouihri et al 2003, PMID 12864857).

machinery_genes.quote: MxiK and MxiN interact with the Spa47 ATPase and are required for transit of the needle components MxiH and MxiI
machinery_genes.note: MxiK = SctK/OrgA-family C-ring base; required for externalization of needle subunits. Also named in Schroeder & Hilbi 2008 (PMID 18202440).

machinery_genes.role: ATPase stator / C-ring link (couples Spa47 to C-ring)
machinery_genes.quote: MxiK and MxiN interact with the Spa47 ATPase and are required for transit of the needle components MxiH and MxiI
machinery_genes.note: MxiN = SctL/YscL/OrgB-family ATPase stator linking Spa47 to the C-ring.

machinery_genes.quote: Spa32, which determines the length of the T3SS needle

machinery_genes.role: needle-tip complex protein (secretion plug / translocon assembly)
machinery_genes.quote: a conformational change in the "plug" protein IpaD, which also leads to the repositioning of IpaB and allows its passage and membrane insertion along with the second hydrophobic translocator protein IpaC, thus forming the multimeric translocation pore
machinery_genes.note: IpaD = needle-tip 'plug'; with IpaB/IpaC forms the host-membrane translocon. Counted as apparatus (translocon), not effector, per apparatus brief.

machinery_genes.quote: the repositioning of IpaB and allows its passage and membrane insertion along with the second hydrophobic translocator protein IpaC, thus forming the multimeric translocation pore
machinery_genes.note: IpaB = hydrophobic translocator forming the multimeric translocation pore; apparatus, not effector, per brief.

machinery_genes.quote: along with the second hydrophobic translocator protein IpaC, thus forming the multimeric translocation pore
machinery_genes.note: IpaC = second hydrophobic translocator of the host-membrane pore; apparatus (translocon), not effector, per brief.

notes: Mxi-Spa is the archetypal Shigella flexneri injectisome (T3SS) encoded on the ~220 kb virulence plasmid pWR100/pWR501; in S. flexneri 2a 301 (NC_004851.1 = the pWR501 virulence plasmid). Assembly requires ~20 mxi and spa genes (Schroeder & Hilbi 2008, PMID 18202440). Core apparatus fully covered by literature with verbatim naming quotes: secretin (MxiD) + pilotin (MxiM); basal-body IM/periplasmic rings (MxiG SctD, MxiJ SctJ); needle (MxiH SctF) + inner rod (MxiI SctI); IM export gate (MxiA SctV, Spa24 SctR, Spa9 SctS, Spa29 SctT, Spa40 SctU); cytoplasmic sorting platform/C-ring + ATPase (Spa47 SctN, Spa33 SctQ, MxiK SctK, MxiN SctL); needle-length ruler (Spa32 SctP); and the IpaB/IpaC/IpaD tip-translocon (counted as apparatus per the T3SS brief, since the translocators form the host-membrane pore). The Sct family assignments come from the apparatus brief vocabulary; the synonym-to-Spa mappings (Spa24=SctR, Spa9=SctS, Spa29=SctT, Spa40=SctU) follow the brief's Shigella column. Spa9/Spa24/Spa29 are named only collectively in the IM-ring sentence (no individual functional-characterization quote found here), so their precise gate-family role is the lowest-confidence call in this set, but their membership in the apparatus is quoted. Deliberately EXCLUDED as non-machinery: secreted effectors (the 24 curated Ipa/IpaH/Osp/VirA effectors for this instance), the translocator/effector chaperones IpgC, IpgE, Spa15 and Spa13 (Spa13 is an escort/switch regulator, not a structural channel), the gatekeeper MxiC, and transcriptional regulators MxiE/VirF/VirB/InvE. MxiC (SctW gatekeeper) is a genuine apparatus component but no clean verbatim naming quote was captured, so it was omitted rather than padded. All quotes are verbatim from the cited PubMed/PMC sources.
